data.title: Vera Bradley outlines Project Sunshine transformation with focus on product innovation and brand clarity
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Vera Bradley (VRA) Q3 2026

Management View

  • Interim CEO Ian Bickley provided an update on Vera Bradley's transformation, stating, "I remain confident that with the right focus, effort and execution, we have a tremendous opportunity to increase market share and return the business to growth by reengaging our loyal customer base while also expanding our reach and relevance to new customer segments." Bickley highlighted the launch of Project Sunshine, a branded strategy built around five transformation pillars: sharpening brand focus, resetting the go-to-market approach, rewiring the digital ecosystem, implementing Outlet 2.0, and reimagining organizational operations.

  • Bickley emphasized sequential improvements: "For the third quarter, we registered revenues of $62.3 million, 11.7% below prior year. This compared to a 24.6% decline during the second quarter. Revenues in our direct business segment were $49.7 million, 5.3% below prior year compared to a decline of 16.2% in the second quarter. Importantly, we achieved sequential improvement in our key metrics in nearly all direct segment channels, highlighted by positive comparable channel sales in our brand channels that have been product-led and have continued for 5 months, extending from back-to-school through the Black Friday weekend."

  • Bickley detailed product performance highlights, including the success of heritage-inspired prints, the Original 100 Bag, strong Gen Z engagement, and a collaboration with Anthropologie. The company is expanding commitments to these products for spring/summer '26 and relaunching the Hathaway Tote.

  • Regarding the CEO search, Bickley stated, "We continue to be focused on finding the right future leader for Vera Bradley. It's a critical decision for the business that we want to get right. While we do not have any updates currently, we are moving forward rapidly with Project Sunshine and shoring up key leadership positions across the business, including the recent appointment of our Chief Brand Officer."

  • CFO Martin Layding reported, "For the third quarter of fiscal 2026, our consolidated revenues totaled $62.3 million compared to $70.5 million in the prior year third quarter. Net loss from continuing operations for the third quarter totaled negative $8.3 million or negative $0.30 per diluted share compared to negative $3.7 million last year or negative $0.13 per diluted share. Results from continuing operations for the quarter were significantly affected by a $5.9 million inventory write-down related to the brand's strategic product shift toward cotton and heritage prints, along with a $4 million write-off of television media credits." Layding also noted a 24.3% year-over-year inventory decrease.

Outlook

  • Management did not provide explicit guidance figures but reiterated a focus on sequential improvement, product innovation, disciplined pricing, and cost controls as key drivers for upcoming quarters. Bickley stated, "We are pleased with the progress we are making with Project Sunshine and expect the cumulative impact of these initiatives to continue to positively impact the momentum of our business going forward."

Financial Results

  • Consolidated revenues for the third quarter were $62.3 million. Direct segment revenues reached $49.7 million, with comparable sales declining 5.8%, marking the third quarter of sequential improvement. Indirect segment revenues were $12.6 million, a 30.2% decrease from the prior year. Gross margin was $26 million or 41.7% of net revenues, impacted by inventory write-downs; excluding these, gross margin was 51.2%. SG&A expenses were $37.4 million, and operating loss from continuing operations was negative $11.1 million. Inventory at quarter-end was $82.9 million, down from $109.6 million the prior year.

Q&A

  • Eric Beder, Small Cap Consumer Research, LLC: "When we roll into 2026, what should we be thinking about as the kind of the key signpost that Project Sunshine is starting to have an even greater impact than it had in Q3 and into Q4?" Bickley: "I believe that success with product will be the most important thing that can turn the business. And frankly, is the sort of positive experience that we're having in our brand channels right now, I believe, is primarily product-led."

  • Beder: "When you look at Outlet 2.0... what's kind of been the response to consumers to seeing kind of full-price items in the Outlet 2.0 stores. And when you look at it, does that become a bridge given that a lot of -- there's been a lot of closures in the full-price stores?" Bickley: "On Outlet 2.0, it's early days. We launched 7 pilot stores this holiday season. I would say that the qualitative feedback that we're getting from our teams as well as customers has been very positive... we're seeing very encouraging reaction to customers on the limited assortment of heritage product and select IP that we put into these stores."

  • Beder: "How should we be thinking about the opportunities... to capture working capital and get more productive going forward with the inventory?" Layding: "On inventory, we definitely see the opportunity for improvement there and to improve our -- from a productivity standpoint. And today, our turns are less than 2, but we have seen -- we're starting to see the improvement in turns this quarter, and we think that we're on track through our planning processes and other activities we're taking on to kind of move that into the greater than 2 to 3 range over the course of the next 12 to 18 months."

  • Beder: "How long a journey do you think it can be to go a little find that younger customer?" Bickley: "It will take some time... But even that, we saw twice the penetration of Gen Z customers on that bag that we have across other products in the range."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for evidence that transformation initiatives are gaining traction, focusing on signposts for Project Sunshine's impact and the company’s ability to attract younger customers. The tone was slightly positive but cautious, with pointed questions about inventory improvement and channel transitions.

  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone during both prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing early progress and positive feedback from initial pilots. Bickley frequently referenced confidence in product-led growth and operational improvements, while Layding outlined clear inventory targets.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, Q&A was more detailed and forward-focused, with analysts seeking proof points and management responding with data-supported optimism.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter emphasized Project Sunshine as the branded transformation strategy, compared to the previous quarter’s reference to foundational strategic initiatives. The focus shifted toward specific product successes, digital engagement, and a more granular approach to inventory management.

  • Analysts in both quarters sought clarity on turnaround milestones and execution risks, but this quarter's questions were more targeted on operational KPIs and new initiatives like Outlet 2.0.

  • Key financial metrics showed sequential improvement, with revenues declining at a slower pace and inventory levels reduced further compared to Q2. Management’s tone was more upbeat, pointing to early wins and greater confidence in the transformation strategy.

  • Strategic priorities evolved from general brand sharpening and omnichannel development to a more cohesive, branded transformation effort anchored by Project Sunshine, with a greater focus on product innovation and organizational agility.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited challenges related to inventory write-downs, shifting consumer dynamics, and the need for continued execution of transformation initiatives. The $5.9 million inventory write-down and $4 million media credit write-off were highlighted as significant one-time items impacting earnings.

  • Analysts raised concerns about the pace of inventory productivity improvements and the timeline for attracting younger customers.

  • The company is mitigating risks through disciplined planning, data-driven decision-making, and ongoing cost control efforts.

Final Takeaway

Vera Bradley management remains focused on delivering a holistic transformation through Project Sunshine, emphasizing product innovation, brand clarity, and operational discipline. Early progress in key channels, sequential improvement in financial metrics, and positive responses to new initiatives provide a foundation for cautious optimism as the company continues to execute its turnaround strategy.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Starbucks worker's union stages 'Downtown Throwdown' in Chicago
data.description:
The Starbucks Workers United ((SWU)) strike continues to grind on with the action escalating to a 48-hour demonstration at the company’s locations across Chicago.

The “Downtown Throwdown” will spread to over a dozen restaurants located in Chicago’s Loop and River North, including Chicago’s Reserve Roastery, the world’s largest Starbucks. Demonstrations will run through the day and night until Friday morning in what the union characterizes as “the boldest demonstrations yet of union baristas’ resolve.”

The “Red Cup Rebellion” kicked off mid-November to coincide with the company’s busiest day of the year, spreading to 95 stores in 65 cities within a week. The strike has since included 3,000 baristas across more than 145 stores in 105 cities, making this the longest, nationwide unfair labor practice strike in the company’s history.

The strike has also drawn solidarity from trade unions across the globe as the “No Contract, No Coffee” campaign resulted in demonstrations in South America, Europe and Asia.

In the company’s hometown, three stores currently remain closed due to staffing shortages resulting from the strike, including the iconic Starbucks Reserve Roastery in Seattle’s Capitol Hill neighborhood. While the company states the store has been closed since September as part of a major corporate restructuring and store consolidation, pressure from the union likely contributed to the decision to permanently shutter the restaurant.

To end the action, the union is demanding Starbucks (SBUX) offer employees a new contract that would provide for better hours to improve staffing, higher take-home pay, and a resolution of unfair labor practices by the company.

“Finalizing a fair union contract would cost Starbucks less than one average day’s sales and less than Starbucks CEO Brian Niccol’s $96 million compensation for just four months of work in 2024,” the union said.

Although the SWU says the company has not attempted to negotiate an end to the strike, Starbucks (SBUX) claims the union walked away from talks while also arguing that it already provides top retail employment with an average hourly compensation exceeding $30 in pay and benefits.

Despite attempts to disrupt the business and put a meaningful dent in sales, the strike has so far done seemingly little financial harm to Starbucks (SBUX). This year’s Red Cup Day was the strongest in the company’s history in terms of sales and store traffic, and management previously stated that the strikes haven’t significantly disrupted operations nor impacted the company’s bottom-line.

Seeking Alpha has reached out to Starbucks for comment regarding the Red Cup Rebellion and any recent impact on the company's business.

data.title: Nasdaq falls while the Dow climbs after Oracle’s post-earnings drop
data.description:
Wall Street trades mixed on Thursday as the tech-oriented Nasdaq Composite drags lower after Oracle (ORCL) posted mixed results, pressuring the AI trade.

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) tumbled the hardest as it lost 1%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) pushed down by 0.3% while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) climbed up by 1%.

Across sectors, seven of the 11 S&P groups are in positive territory led by Materials. At the other end of the spectrum, Info Tech has been the weakest performer so far.

Investors also continue to react to Wednesday’s Fed decision to cut its policy rate by 25 basis points to 3.50%-3.75%, which is now its lowest level in three years. 

“The Federal Reserve sent a clear message that the Fed is in the midst of an accommodative phase, largely driven by the recent weakness in the labor market. While 2026 may not bring as many rate cuts as 2025, it's clear that the Fed's prior hiking cycle that disrupted markets and the economy post-Covid is firmly in the rearview mirror, and that's bullish for stocks,” Chris Kampitsis, managing partner of Barnum Financial Group said. 

Furthermore, Deutsche Bank’s Jim Reid stated: “Bond yields have also moved lower, which partly reflects the Fed and the wider risk-off tone this morning.” Reid noted Japan’s robust bond auction and Australia’s unexpected employment contraction as contributing factors. 

Yields were lower across the curve, with the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) falling 3 basis points to 3.51% and the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dropping 4 basis points to 4.11%. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Oracle (ORCL) dropped 13.6% while shares of Mosaic (MOS) pushed up by 6.8%.

data.title: Tencent said to have poached ByteDance's researchers to beef up AI talent
data.description:
According to a report by The Information on Thursday, China's Tencent has been engaged in an aggressive poaching campaign to lure AI researchers from ByteDance (BDNCE) with double salaries, and the company has successfully hired at least some of them.

Sources told the tech news outlet that Tencent has also offered some fresh doctorate graduates salaries 50% higher than the industry standard as it bolsters its AI talent.

The report said Tencent reorganized its AI development team, demoting leaders with little experience in research, after its HY AI models largely failed to make a dent in the market and made it look like a laggard in the industry.

Tencent’s lag could be attributed to the fact that the company hasn’t historically focused as much on research as other companies, the report said.

The report said Tencent's HY team structure was "an issue." Last year, HY operated as a loosely defined organization scattered across multiple departments, two Tencent employees told The Info. The fragmentation sometimes led to internal rivalry as different AI model projects competed. That resulted in a waste of resources, the employees said.

Sources told The Info that senior Tencent executives grew increasingly aware that the ability to develop strong in-house AI models is critical to the company’s long-term success. Earlier this year, the HY team came under intense pressure from the company’s president, Martin Lau, to step up its game.

To do so, the company hired OpenAI’s (OPENAI) Yao Shunyu with the mission to overhaul the HY team and recruit talent, sources told the news outlet. Yao quickly got busy trying to poach talent from elsewhere, including from ByteDance.

In addition to offering higher pay, Tencent pitched them on flexibility to pursue their research ideas, a flat hierarchy, and opportunities to make a bigger impact given that its AI team is going through a makeover, the report said.

data.title: Thermo Fisher upgraded at Citi on boost in pharma spending
data.description:
• Citi upgraded Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) to buy from neutral as the life sciences tools company should benefit from increases in pharma company R&D spending as that industry has more clarity on drug pricing following several deals with the Trump administration.
  • The bank raised its price target to $660 from $580 (~15% upside based on Dec. 10 close).
  • Analyst Patrick Donnelly said that Thermo Fisher is "at the forefront of Tools companies that stand to benefit" from the spending boost.
  • He added that he has been hearing that "Pharma is increasingly optimistic on its abilities to navigate headwinds that were previously considered uncertain."

data.title: Investors nibble on restaurant stocks after a year of weak sector performance
data.description:
The restaurant sector was a mild outperformer on Thursday, a day after the Federal Reserve cut its key overnight borrowing rate by a quarter percentage point to a range of 3.50% to 3.75% and signaled a slower pace of rate cuts ahead.

The AdvisorShares Restaurant ETF (EATZ) was up 1.6% in contrast to the S&P 500 Index, which was down 0.2% in early action. Notable gainers included Brinker International (EAT) +6.2%, Cracker Barrel (CBRL) +4.6%, Yum! Brands (YUM) +3.7%, First Watch Restaurant Group (FWRG) +3.6%, Wendy's (WEN) +3.4%, Shake Shack (SHAK) +2.7%, and Bloomin' Brands (BLMN) +2.4%.

Restaurant stocks have lagged broader retail this year by a significant margin, primarily because traffic and same-store sales have decelerated while costs for labor, food, rent, and interest remain at elevated levels to pressure margins. At the same time, consumers have shifted toward value formats and at-home consumption due to affordability issues, so many restaurant concepts look more cyclical to investors and less defensible than other retail categories. The AI trade is also less relevant for restaurant chains in comparison to other retail categories that have seen an immediate boost in e-commerce conversions and efficient benefits in operations and marketing that have already had a profound impact on quarterly earnings and guidance updates. While restaurant companies are doing what they can to leverage generative AI, the story of its upside has been less appetizing to analysts and investors.

The Dow Jones US Restaurants & Bars Index (DJUSRU) is still down 2.5% for the year, despite the mini-rally on Thursday.

data.title: Alcon draws double downgrade at BofA on earnings growth risk
data.description:
Alcon (ALC) received its lone sell-equivalent rating on Wall Street as Bank of America downgraded the Swiss eye care company to Underperform from Buy on Thursday, arguing that consensus estimates are overly optimistic and the company’s facing earnings downside risk.

Analyst Julien Ouaddour noted that amid worsening investor sentiment, Geneva-based Alcon (ALC) has lost ~15% in 2026, a year characterized by a market slowdown, rivalry, and guidance cuts.

While investors expect a rebound and market share gains into next year, Ouaddour argues that there is not enough room for “positive surprises” amid slower adoption of the Unity systems, Alcon's surgical platforms for cataract and vitreoretinal procedures.

According to the analyst, the company has lowered its projection for annual installations of Unity systems to roughly 2,400 and 3,000 for 2026 and 2027, respectively, compared to 3,000 and 4,000 previously.

“The Street assumes a sharp rebound, share gains, and an ambitious Unity ramp-up despite low visibility, leaving little room for positive surprises,” he wrote, cutting ALC’s price target to CHF60 ($75) from CHF80 ($100).

“While investor conversations point to improving confidence into 2026 – on hopes of market recovery and earnings acceleration—we believe consensus expectations are too optimistic,” Ouaddour wrote.

data.title: Chubb deal for AIG `very unlikely,' analyst says
data.description:
A Chubb (CB) deal for AIG (AIG) appears "very unlikely" after a report on Wednesday that Chubb made an informal takeover approach to AIG, according to a Cantor Fitzgerald analyst.

"We view a marriage of AIG and Chubb - or any other suitor for that matter - as very unlikely," Cantor analyst Ryan Tunis wrote in a note on Wednesday.

Chubb (CB), during the last month. reached out to AIG (AIG) to indicate its willingness to do a deal, Insurance Insider reported on Wednesday. Chubb denied that it made an offer to AIG and AIG said it's not for sale.

AIG and Chubb have "way too much overlap" as primary insurers in the multinational insurance world, according to Tunis. 

"Deal logic here wouldn't make sense for Chubb and we think the stock would be punished significantly, even if the price were at last sale price," Tunis wrote.

A Piper Sandler analyst sees a potential combination of Chubb (CB) and AIG as possible, especially given the firm's relationship with the Greenberg family. Hank Greenberg was the long-term CEO of AIG and his son Evan Greenberg, is the CEO of Chubb and worked at AIG for many years. 

"We see the merger as possible, but we recommend investors take care when adding take-out speculation to their investment thesis," Piper Sandler analyst Paul Newsome, wrote in a note on Thursday.

Shares of AIG rose 3.3% on Thursday after gaining 6% on Wednesday in the wake of the Chubb speculation. Chubb advanced 3%.

data.title: Schneider Electric extends revenue growth forecast to 7% to 10%
data.description:
Schneider Electric (SBGSF) (SBGSY) raised its medium-term outlook on Thursday and outlined a substantial share repurchase plan that could reach $4.09 billion by the end of the decade.

During its capital markets day, the French industrial group said it now expects annual organic revenue growth of 7% to 10% from 2026 through 2030, effectively extending its current guidance. The company also projects that its adjusted ebitda margin will improve by a cumulative 250 basis points over the same period, with cash conversion holding near 100%.

Schneider Electric (SBGSF) (SBGSY) additionally announced a planned share buyback program of €2.5 billion to €3.5 billion ($2.92 billion to $4.09 billion) running through 2030, alongside a targeted divestment effort involving €1 billion to €1.5 billion in revenue to be completed by the end of the decade.

data.title: Atossa gains on Rare Pediatric Disease status for Duchenne asset
data.description:
• Atossa Therapeutics (ATOS) is up ~11% in Thursday trading after reporting that the US FDA has granted Rare Pediatric Disease designation to Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy candidate "Z"-Endoxifen.
  • If "Z"-Endoxifen is eventually approved, Atossa would be eligible for a Priority Review voucher that could be sold to another company.
  • "Z"-Endoxifen is a selective estrogen receptor modulator that Atossa says has 100 times greater potency than existing SERMs with better tolerability. It is also under investigation for breast cancer.
